segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a dynamic short track speed skating race from the Torino 2006 Winter Olympics, showing skaters in tight competition, with Skater 370 in a red suit and yellow helmet leading a group, all set to an upbeat, driving electronic music track. The music fades slightly with a distinct 'whoosh' sound as the scene transitions to a title card reading 'LAST 5 MEN'S 500M SHORT TRACK SPEED SKATING.' The focus then shifts to the Salt Lake City 2002 Winter Olympics, where Canadian skater Marc Gagnon, wearing a red and black suit with a yellow helmet marked '315,' is shown celebrating after a race, accompanied by loud, enthusiastic crowd cheering and a muffled celebratory announcement. The video then replays the final laps of his 500m race, with on-screen graphics indicating laps remaining and elapsed time. Gagnon, in red, is seen maneuvering aggressively, overtaking competitors in black and blue suits, and ultimately crossing the finish line first as the timer shows 'Finish' and the Seiko logo appears. Throughout this intense replay, the arena is filled with the continuous, building roar of spectators, highlighting the dramatic conclusion of the race, with the Olympic rings and 'SALT LAKE 2002' branding visible.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a dynamic short track speed skating race from the Torino 2006 Winter Olympics, showing skaters in tight competition, with one in a red suit and yellow helmet (number 370) leading a group. The scene transitions to a title card reading 'LAST 5 MEN'S 500M SHORT TRACK SPEED SKATING,' followed by a shift to the Salt Lake City 2002 Winter Olympics. The focus narrows to Canadian skater Marc Gagnon, wearing a red and black suit with a yellow helmet marked '315,' who is shown celebrating after a race. The video then replays the final laps of his 500m race, with on-screen graphics indicating laps remaining and elapsed time. Gagnon, in red, is seen maneuvering aggressively, overtaking competitors in black and blue suits, and ultimately crossing the finish line first as the timer shows 'Finish' and the Seiko logo appears. The arena is filled with spectators, and the Olympic rings and 'SALT LAKE 2002' branding are visible throughout.

audio_updated_entities.Marc Gagnon: Canadian short track speed skater wearing a red and black suit with a yellow helmet marked '315'; seen celebrating after a race and racing in the 500m event at Salt Lake City 2002, with his celebration marked by loud crowd cheering and a muffled celebratory announcement.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a thrilling moment from the Salt Lake City 2002 Winter Olympics short track speed skating 500m event, as Marc Gagnon (Skater 315) and Skater 316, both in red and black Canadian suits with yellow helmets, cross the finish line to the immediate, roaring sound of an ecstatic crowd. The finish time of 41.81 seconds is displayed as Gagnon, wearing helmet '315', raises his arms in triumph, his victory met with sustained cheers. The two skaters embrace joyfully, celebrating with wide smiles and raised fists, their excitement amplified by the crowd's roar. Gagnon then removes his helmet, revealing his blonde hair, waves to the crowd, and hoists the Canadian flag high above his head, all while the celebratory crowd noise continues. An announcer's voice clearly exclaims "Gagnon!" as the scene transitions to dynamic race footage. Here, Gagnon (315) and Skater 316 (316) are seen in intense competition, maneuvering closely with other skaters including Skater 370 (in red suit, helmet '370'), a skater in a black suit with a yellow helmet, and a skater in a blue suit with a yellow helmet. This fast-paced jostling for position on the ice is underscored by an upbeat, triumphant background music track, accompanied by the distinct sounds of skates on ice. The video concludes with Gagnon, now without his helmet, raising his arms in victory again, his face beaming with emotion as he celebrates his Olympic success under the 'SALT LAKE 2002' banner and Olympic rings, with spectators visible in the stands, and the announcer's voice emphatically declaring "Marc Gagnon!" amidst renewed crowd cheering.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video captures a thrilling moment from the Salt Lake City 2002 Winter Olympics short track speed skating 500m event. The sequence begins with Marc Gagnon (Skater 315) and Skater 316, both in red and black Canadian suits with yellow helmets, crossing the finish line in a close race. Gagnon, wearing helmet '315', raises his arms in triumph as the finish time of 41.81 seconds is displayed on screen. The two skaters embrace joyfully, celebrating their victory with wide smiles and raised fists. Gagnon then removes his helmet, revealing his blonde hair, and waves to the crowd before hoisting the Canadian flag high above his head. The scene transitions to race footage showing Gagnon (315) and Skater 316 (316) in intense competition, maneuvering closely with other skaters including Skater 370 (in red suit, helmet '370'), a skater in a black suit with yellow helmet, and a skater in a blue suit with yellow helmet. The race is fast-paced, with skaters jostling for position on the ice. The video concludes with Gagnon, now without his helmet, raising his arms in victory again, his face beaming with emotion as he celebrates his Olympic success. The background features the 'SALT LAKE 2002' banner and Olympic rings, with spectators visible in the stands, adding to the celebratory atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a triumphant scene from the Salt Lake City 2002 Winter Olympics, accompanied by a roaring crowd. Canadian short track speed skater Marc Gagnon, in his red and black suit with yellow helmet '315', stands on the podium, arms raised, waving the Canadian flag. He is joined by Skater 316, who also celebrates with a raised fist, and they embrace in victory amidst the cheers. The scene then transitions to the Torino 2006 Winter Olympics, where the crowd's excitement builds, culminating in an announcer's enthusiastic declaration of "Apolo Anton Ohno!" Apolo Anton Ohno, the American skater in a blue suit with a red bandana and yellow helmet, is seen preparing for the Men's 500m Short Track Speed Skating event. A sharp starting sound signals the race's commencement, and Ohno, along with competitors in red and blue suits, launches from the starting line as the crowd erupts. As the race progresses, the announcer tracks Ohno's position, noting he is "on the first turn," then "second," "third," and later "still in fourth" with "three laps to go." Skaters navigate tight turns and maintain close proximity, with the on-screen timer tracking their progress, all against a backdrop of continuous, building crowd noise. The race intensifies in the final laps, with the crowd audibly chanting "Ohno! Ohno!" as the announcer exclaims "One lap to go! Ohno!" Ohno makes a strategic move to overtake rivals, including a skater in a red suit and another in a blue suit, approaching the finish line. The atmosphere is electric, with the crowd visibly engaged and roaring as the announcer shouts "Oh my god! Ohno! He wins! He wins!" Ohno crosses the line first, securing his victory in a close finish.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video begins with a celebratory moment from the Salt Lake City 2002 Winter Olympics, where Marc Gagnon, the Canadian short track speed skater in a red and black suit with yellow helmet '315', stands triumphantly on the podium, arms raised, waving the Canadian flag. He is joined by Skater 316, who also celebrates with a raised fist, and they embrace in victory. The scene then transitions to the Torino 2006 Winter Olympics, focusing on Apolo Anton Ohno, the American skater in a blue suit with a red bandana and yellow helmet, preparing for the Men's 500m Short Track Speed Skating event. The race begins with Ohno and other skaters, including competitors in red and blue suits, launching from the starting line. As the race progresses, the skaters navigate tight turns and maintain close proximity, with the on-screen timer tracking their progress. The race intensifies in the final laps, with Ohno making a strategic move to overtake rivals, including a skater in a red suit and another in a blue suit, as they approach the finish line. The crowd is visibly engaged, and the atmosphere is electric, culminating in a close finish with Ohno crossing the line first, securing his victory.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a thrilling visual of Apolo Anton Ohno, in his blue USA suit and yellow helmet '254', dramatically crossing the finish line of the Men's 500m Short Track Speed Skating event at Torino 2006, his arm raised in triumph as the timer flashes 41.93 seconds. A massive crowd roar and distinct "whoop" erupts, accompanying his joyful skate along the rink, waving to spectators with a wide smile, his red bandana visible. As he continues to celebrate, raising both arms and pointing skyward in elation, an announcer with an Italian accent exclaims, "Apolo Anton Ohno..." The scene then cuts to a replay of the final stretch, set to upbeat, triumphant background music, showing Ohno in a tight race with the Skater in red suit ('211') and the Skater in blue suit ('239'). Another announcer, also with an Italian accent, provides commentary, stating "E' il primo, è il primo... Apolo Anton Ohno, il primo..." as the replay highlights Ohno's decisive overtake and aggressive skating style. The crowd's cheers intensify as Ohno crosses the line again in the replay, raising his arms in victory with the Olympic rings and 'Torino 2006' branding visible. The commentary continues, describing him having "crossed the finish line." The video culminates with Ohno embracing the moment and the crowd's energy, as the first announcer emphatically shouts, "APOLO ANTON OHNO!" followed by an overwhelming roar from the ecstatic crowd, perfectly capturing the palpable Olympic atmosphere.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video captures a thrilling moment from the Men's 500m Short Track Speed Skating event at the Torino 2006 Winter Olympics. The sequence begins with Apolo Anton Ohno, in his blue USA suit and yellow helmet marked '254', crossing the finish line in a dramatic victory, raising his arm in triumph as the timer shows 41.93 seconds. He skates joyfully along the rink, waving to the crowd and celebrating with a wide smile, his red bandana visible beneath his helmet. The camera follows him as he continues to celebrate, raising both arms and pointing skyward, clearly elated. The scene then cuts to a replay of the final stretch, showing Ohno in a tight race with the Skater in red suit (yellow helmet '211') and the Skater in blue suit (yellow helmet '239'), with Ohno making a decisive move to overtake. The replay highlights the intense competition and Ohno’s aggressive skating style. As he crosses the line again in the replay, he raises his arms in victory, with the Olympic rings and 'Torino 2006' branding visible in the background. The crowd, including photographers and spectators, erupts in cheers, capturing the moment. The video concludes with Ohno continuing his celebration, embracing the moment and the crowd’s energy, with the Olympic atmosphere palpable throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with triumphant orchestral music and a roaring crowd, as a jubilant Charles Hamelin, wearing a red bandana, blue fur-lined jacket, and a gold medal, celebrates his victory with raised arms, a wide smile, and a bouquet of flowers, looking emotionally upwards. The scene transitions to a wide shot of the Vancouver 2010 Winter Olympics short track speed skating arena, with on-screen text confirming the event as 'CHARLES HAMELIN MEN'S 500M SHORT TRACK SPEED SKATING'. Anticipatory crowd murmurs give way to an announcer's excited shout of "Charles Hamelin!" followed by a massive roar and a whistle, as the video cuts to the intense race. Skaters in red (Hamelin, 205), blue (244), and light blue (256) suits with numbered helmets compete fiercely, tightly grouped and leaning into turns on the ice, with '4 LAPS TO GO' displayed. Fast-paced commentary describes the action, highlighting Hamelin's performance: "Hamelin in the lead!", "Hamelin is flying!", as he takes the lead, jostling for position with other competitors (including skater 208 in red). The crowd's excitement builds with each lap ('3 LAPS TO GO', '2 LAPS TO GO'), culminating in the commentator's declaration, "Hamelin is going to win!", "He's done it!", "Gold medal!", "Charles Hamelin!". The segment concludes with a massive roar of celebration and triumphant music, echoing Hamelin's initial jubilant display.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a jubilant athlete wearing a red bandana with white patterns, a blue jacket with a fur-lined hood, and a gold medal around his neck, celebrating with raised arms and a wide smile. He holds a bouquet of flowers and looks upward with emotion, clearly savoring his victory. The scene transitions to a wide shot of the short track speed skating arena during the Vancouver 2010 Winter Olympics, with the on-screen text identifying the event as 'CHARLES HAMELIN MEN'S 500M SHORT TRACK SPEED SKATING'. The camera then cuts to the race itself, showing skaters in red, blue, and light blue suits with numbered helmets (e.g., 205, 244, 256) competing fiercely on the ice. The skaters are tightly grouped, leaning into turns with intense focus, as the race progresses through laps marked by on-screen timers and lap indicators ('4 LAPS TO GO', '3 LAPS TO GO', '2 LAPS TO GO'). The crowd is visible in the background, and the Olympic rings and 'Vancouver 2010' branding are prominent on the rink barriers. The skaters maintain high speed, jostling for position, with one in a red suit (Charles Hamelin) appearing to take the lead as the race nears its conclusion.

audio_updated_entities.Charles Hamelin: Canadian short track speed skater, identified by commentators and the roaring crowd as the winner, wearing a red and black suit with a white helmet marked '205' during the Men's 500m event at Vancouver 2010, and later seen jubilantly celebrating his gold medal victory with a red bandana, blue jacket, and bouquet of flowers.

segments.integrated_caption: The video captures the intense final moments of the Men's 500m short track speed skating final at the Vancouver 2010 Winter Olympics, set against the deafening roar of an electric crowd and the urgent, highly excited commentary. A tight pack of skaters, including Charles Hamelin (CAN, #205 in red and black with a white helmet), Skater in blue suit (244, KOREA, in blue with white helmet), Skater in light blue suit (256, in light blue with white helmet), and Skater in red suit (208, CAN, in red with white helmet), lean low and accelerate around the brightly lit Olympic rink, with 'Vancouver 2010' branding visible. As the final lap begins, the commentators' voices rise in pitch, describing the jostling for position. Hamelin makes a decisive move, with commentators exclaiming "Hamelin!" and "He's done it!" as he overtakes Skater 244 on the inside. The camera follows the action closely, capturing the physicality and speed as Hamelin crosses the finish line first, his arms raised in triumph, to a thunderous roar from the crowd and jubilant shouts from the commentators confirming "Gold medal!" for Canada and announcing his time of "40.981". Immediately, upbeat, celebratory music begins to play as Hamelin is joined by Skater 208, who also celebrates with a Canadian flag, and they embrace joyfully amidst the sustained cheers and patriotic atmosphere. On-screen graphics visually confirm Hamelin as the winner with the time of 40.981 seconds, solidifying the national pride and athletic achievement.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video captures the intense final moments of the Men's 500m short track speed skating final at the Vancouver 2010 Winter Olympics. The race begins with a tight pack of skaters, including Charles Hamelin (CAN, #205 in red and black with a white helmet), Skater in blue suit (244, KOREA, in blue with white helmet), Skater in light blue suit (256, in light blue with white helmet), and Skater in red suit (208, CAN, in red with white helmet), all leaning low and accelerating around the rink. As the final lap begins, the skaters jostle for position, with Hamelin making a decisive move to overtake Skater 244 on the inside. The camera follows the action closely, capturing the physicality and speed as Hamelin crosses the finish line first, his arms raised in triumph. He is immediately joined by Skater 208, who also celebrates with a Canadian flag, and they embrace joyfully. The on-screen graphics confirm Hamelin as the winner with a time of 40.981 seconds. The crowd erupts in cheers, and the atmosphere is electric with national pride and athletic achievement. The setting is the brightly lit Olympic arena, with 'Vancouver 2010' branding visible on the boards and the Olympic rings displayed prominently.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Charles Hamelin, a Canadian short track speed skater, wearing a red quilted jacket with 'CANADA' emblazoned across the chest and a red, white, and black beanie. He is seen smiling and looking down, then turns to face the crowd, raising his arms in celebration while holding a gold medal and a bouquet of flowers. The crowd behind him is cheering loudly, and a French announcer excitedly exclaims "Charles Hamelin!" followed by more sustained crowd cheers. Triumphant orchestral music plays in the background. A graphic overlay identifies him as 'CHARLES HAMELIN - GOLD - MEN'S 500M'. The scene then transitions to a wide shot of the Sochi 2014 Olympic arena, where the Men's 500m Short Track Speed Skating event is about to begin. The ice rink is surrounded by a large, enthusiastic audience. An English announcer gives official instructions, stating "You have one false start," and then "Go to the start," before declaring "Ready." A sharp buzzer signals the race's commencement. Skaters in red, black, and white suits with blue helmets line up at the starting line in a crouched position. As the race begins, they accelerate rapidly, leaning into the turns with intense focus. The camera follows the pack as they navigate the oval track, with speed indicators showing over 40 km/h. The skaters maintain tight formation, jostling for position, with Viktor Ahn (in a red suit with helmet '212') leading the pack in the early laps. The French announcer provides rapid, excited commentary, repeatedly calling out "Viktor Ahn, Viktor Ahn, Viktor Ahn..." as he maintains his lead. On-screen graphics display '4 LAPS TO GO' and '3 LAPS TO GO' as the race progresses, highlighting the competitive pace and the skaters' strategic movements around the rink.

The scene then transitions to a wide shot of the Sochi 2014 Olympic arena, where the Men's 500m Short Track Speed Skating event is about to begin. The ice rink is surrounded by a large, enthusiastic audience. Skaters in red, black, and white suits with blue helmets line up at the starting line, preparing for the race. The skaters, including Viktor Ahn (in a red suit with helmet '212'), are shown in a crouched position, ready to start. As the race begins, they accelerate rapidly, leaning into the turns with intense focus. The camera follows the pack as they navigate the oval track, with speed indicators showing over 40 km/h. The skaters maintain tight formation, jostling for position, with Viktor Ahn leading the pack in the early laps. The on-screen graphics display '4 LAPS TO GO' and '3 LAPS TO GO' as the race progresses, highlighting the competitive pace and the skaters' strategic movements around the rink.

segments.integrated_caption: The video captures the thrilling final moments of the Men's 500m Short Track Speed Skating event at the Sochi 2014 Winter Olympics, set against a backdrop of loud, excited crowd noise and a rapid, high-energy Russian commentary. Three skaters, including Viktor Ahn in red suit '212', a skater in white suit '250', and another in red suit '203', intensely navigate the ice at high speed with '2 LAPS TO GO' displayed at 23.90 seconds. The crowd's roar intensifies as the skaters jostle for position, with Viktor Ahn taking a decisive lead during the final lap, marked by 'FINAL LAP' and a time of 32.77 seconds. The atmosphere is electric as Viktor Ahn crosses the finish line first at 41.32 seconds, triggering an even louder eruption of cheers and rhythmic sounds from the Russian fans, who wave flags enthusiastically. An English commentator's voice cuts through the celebratory din, declaring Viktor Ahn the "five-time World Champion, the reigning European Champion, and now Olympic Champion in the 500 meters," further fueling the crowd's excitement. Visibly exhausted but triumphant, Ahn skates toward the barrier, where he is embraced and lifted in joy by Russian team members and supporters, many in red and white attire, amidst the continuous, deafening cheers and the visible Sochi 2014 branding and Olympic rings.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video captures the thrilling final moments of the Men's 500m Short Track Speed Skating event at the Sochi 2014 Winter Olympics. The race begins with three skaters—Viktor Ahn in a red suit with blue helmet '212', Skater in white suit '250', and Skater in red suit '203'—intensely competing as they navigate the ice at high speed, with the on-screen timer showing 23.90 seconds and '2 LAPS TO GO'. As the race progresses, the skaters jostle for position, with Viktor Ahn taking the lead during the final lap, marked by the on-screen text 'FINAL LAP' and a time of 32.77 seconds. The pace intensifies, and Viktor Ahn crosses the finish line first, with the timer reading 41.32 seconds and the 'FINISH' marker displayed. The crowd erupts in celebration, with Russian fans waving flags and cheering enthusiastically. Viktor Ahn, visibly exhausted but triumphant, skates toward the barrier, where he is embraced by Russian team members and supporters, who lift him in joy. The scene is filled with emotion as he is hugged and celebrated by teammates and fans, many wearing red and white Russian team attire, with one fan holding a large Russian flag. The atmosphere is electric, with the Sochi 2014 branding and Olympic rings visible throughout the arena, and the OMEGA logo displayed at the finish line.

active_entities.Viktor Ahn: Speed skater in a red suit with a blue helmet marked '212', leading the pack during the final lap, crossing the finish line first, and being celebrated by Russian fans and team members after winning the Men's 500m Short Track Speed Skating event at Sochi 2014.
active_entities.Skater in white suit (250): Competitor in a white suit with a blue helmet marked '250', racing closely with Viktor Ahn and others, finishing second, and later seen celebrating with Russian fans and team members, wearing a white suit with 'RUSSIA' on the back.
active_entities.Skater in red suit (203): Speed skater in a red suit with a blue helmet marked '203', competing closely with Viktor Ahn and Skater in white suit '250', visible during the final stretch, and seen celebrating with Russian supporters after the race.

audio_updated_entities.Viktor Ahn: Speed skater in a red suit with a blue helmet marked '212', leading the pack during the final lap, crossing the finish line first to win the Men's 500m Short Track Speed Skating event at Sochi 2014, and being loudly celebrated by Russian fans and team members as an English commentator declares him the 'five-time World Champion, the reigning European Champion, and now Olympic Champion'.
audio_updated_entities.Russian fans: Enthusiastic spectators in the stands, waving Russian flags, cheering loudly and rhythmically, creating an electric atmosphere of celebration for Viktor Ahn's victory, many wearing red and white team apparel, with their roars intensifying as the race concludes and the victory is announced.
audio_updated_entities.Russian team members: Support staff and coaches in red and white Russian team jackets, seen at the barrier celebrating with Viktor Ahn, embracing him, and lifting him in triumph amidst the roaring crowd and continuous cheers after his victory.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a surge of intense crowd cheering and applause, as Viktor Ahn, in his white and blue Russian suit and light blue helmet marked '212', celebrates his Sochi 2014 Winter Olympics Men's 500m gold medal. He exchanges high-fives and smiles with enthusiastic Russian fans and team members at the barrier, including a young child. The rhythmic crowd noise continues as he skates onto the ice, triumphantly unfurling and waving a large Russian flag, emblazoned with 'СПАСИБО' (Thank You) in red. He then appears on the podium, still waving to the roaring crowd, now wearing a Russian team jacket with his gold medal, as an announcer's voice emphatically declares, "Viktor Ahn!". The scene transitions to the PyeongChang 2018 Winter Olympics, with an aerial night view of the Gangneung Ice Arena, accompanied by a rhythmic countdown music track. Wu Dajing, the Chinese skater in a red and black suit with gold stars and a red helmet, is then seen waving to the crowd as an announcer loudly introduces him, "Wu Dajing!". The race begins with a burst of speed and intense English commentary, as Wu Dajing, marked '6', powerfully leads the pack, including skaters in white ('250') and red ('203') suits. The commentator highlights his significant lead and the incredible speed, noting "44.0 km/h" as the on-screen graphics show 4 laps remaining, all against a backdrop of roaring crowd excitement.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a celebratory moment at the Sochi 2014 Winter Olympics, where Viktor Ahn, wearing a white and blue Russian suit with 'RUSSIA' on the back and a light blue helmet marked '212', is greeted by Russian fans and team members at the barrier. He exchanges high-fives and smiles with supporters, including a young child in a colorful jacket. He then skates onto the ice, unfurling a large Russian flag with 'СПАСИБО' (Thank You) written in red on the white stripe, waving it triumphantly as he circles the rink. The scene transitions to the podium, where Viktor Ahn, now in a white and blue Russian team jacket with a gold medal around his neck, waves to the crowd. On-screen text identifies him as 'VICTOR AN' and notes his gold medal in the Men's 500m. The video then cuts to the PyeongChang 2018 Winter Olympics, showing an aerial night view of the Gangneung Ice Arena, followed by Wu Dajing, a Chinese skater in a red and black suit with gold stars and a red helmet, waving to the crowd. The race begins with Wu Dajing, marked as '6', leading a pack of skaters, including competitors in white and red suits, as they navigate the ice at high speed. The on-screen graphics display the race progress, showing Wu Dajing in first place with 4 laps to go, and the speed reaching 44.0 km/h. The atmosphere shifts from the emotional celebration of Viktor Ahn’s victory to the intense, competitive energy of Wu Dajing’s race, highlighting the global prestige of the Men’s 500m Short Track Speed Skating event.

segments.integrated_caption: The thrilling Men's 500m Short Track Speed Skating final at the PyeongChang 2018 Winter Olympics unfolds at the brightly lit Gangneung Ice Arena, filled with the roaring crowd's anticipation. Four skaters, including Wu Dajing (CHN, #6 in red and black), Hwang Dae-heon (KOR, #58 in white and blue), Lim Hyo-jun (KOR, #189 in white and blue), and skater #203, compete fiercely. Wu Dajing takes an early lead, navigating tight turns at speeds up to 50.0 km/h, as on-screen graphics display lap times and '3 LAPS TO GO'. The Korean commentator's voice rises in pitch and speed, excitedly shouting "우다징! (Wu Dajing!)" and mentioning "황대헌 (Hwang Dae-heon)" and "임효준 (Lim Hyo-jun)" as the race progresses through '2 LAPS TO GO' to the 'FINAL LAP'. As Wu Dajing crosses the finish line first, the crowd erupts in a deafening roar, and the Korean commentator reaches a fever pitch, repeatedly exclaiming "우다징!". On-screen graphics confirm his win and a new World Record of 39.584 seconds. Wu Dajing celebrates jubilantly, raising the Chinese flag triumphantly while skating around the rink, his emotional victory captured by the camera. An English commentator then confirms, "And we have a new world record! 39.584!", as upbeat, celebratory background music begins. Replays highlight the intensity, including a near-collision between Wu Dajing and Hwang Dae-heon, all set against the vibrant atmosphere of the arena with its purple 'PyeongChang 2018' barriers.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video captures a thrilling Men's 500m Short Track Speed Skating final at the PyeongChang 2018 Winter Olympics, held at the Gangneung Ice Arena. The race begins with four skaters—Wu Dajing (CHN, #6 in red and black with gold stars), Hwang Dae-heon (KOR, #58 in white and blue), Lim Hyo-jun (KOR, #189 in white and blue), and another skater in red (#203)—competing fiercely on the ice. The skaters navigate tight turns and maintain high speeds, with on-screen graphics showing lap times, speed (up to 50.0 km/h), and positions. Wu Dajing takes the lead early and maintains it through the final lap, crossing the finish line first with a time of 39.584 seconds, setting a World Record (WR). The crowd erupts as Wu Dajing celebrates, raising the Chinese flag triumphantly while skating around the rink. The camera follows his jubilant celebration, capturing his emotional victory. The on-screen graphic confirms his win: 'SHORT TRACK SPEED SKATING MEN'S 500M WINNER - FINAL A CHN WU DAJING WR 39.584'. Replays show close-up action, including a near-collision between Wu Dajing and Hwang Dae-heon, highlighting the intensity of the race. The arena is brightly lit, with purple barriers displaying 'PyeongChang 2018' and Olympic rings, and spectators are visible in the stands, adding to the electric atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a high-speed short track speed skating race at the PyeongChang 2018 Winter Olympics, set to upbeat, fast-paced electronic background music. Chinese skater Wu Dajing, in a red and black suit with gold stars and a red helmet '6', is seen leading the pack with powerful strides on the Gangneung Ice Arena. He is closely pursued by South Korean skaters Hwang Dae-heon (white and blue suit, blue helmet '58') and Lim Hyo-jun (white and blue suit, blue helmet '189'). An excited male commentator's voice describes the intense competition, calling out Wu Dajing's lead and the close pursuit by Hwang Dae-heon and Lim Hyo-jun, referencing lap times and positions. As Wu Dajing crosses the finish line first, a sudden, loud, sustained crowd roar erupts, transitioning into triumphant, orchestral music. The scene then shifts to the podium, where Wu Dajing, now in a red Chinese team jacket, raises his arms in celebration and proudly displays his gold medal against a blue backdrop with Olympic rings. On-screen graphics confirm his victory: 'CHN WU DAJING - GOLD - MEN'S 500M'. He smiles, gestures to the applauding crowd, and holds a small Olympic-branded box, all while the triumphant music continues.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment opens with a high-speed short track speed skating race at the PyeongChang 2018 Winter Olympics, capturing intense competition on the ice. Wu Dajing, the Chinese skater in a red and black suit with gold stars and a red helmet marked '6', is seen leading the pack, leaning low and skating with powerful strides. He is closely followed by South Korean skaters Hwang Dae-heon (white and blue suit, blue helmet '58') and Lim Hyo-jun (white and blue suit, blue helmet '189'), who are vying for position. The race unfolds on the Gangneung Ice Arena, with purple barriers displaying 'PyeongChang 2018' and Olympic rings, and spectators visible in the background. As Wu Dajing crosses the finish line first, he raises his arms in triumph, celebrating his victory with a wide, exuberant gesture. The scene transitions to the podium, where Wu Dajing, now in a red Chinese team jacket with yellow zippers and a small Chinese flag patch, stands against a blue backdrop with Olympic rings. He raises his arms in celebration, then proudly displays his gold medal, holding it up for the camera. On-screen graphics confirm his victory: 'CHN WU DAJING - GOLD - MEN'S 500M'. He smiles, gestures to the crowd, and holds a small box with Olympic branding, embodying the joy and pride of his achievement.
